danger_signals.visual_form: Dense bullet patterns that increase in complexity across loops; denser fire when enemies are destroyed
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat level and stage difficulty progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Stage-end boss battles that must be defeated to progress
danger_signals.meaning: Critical danger threshold; player must overcome major threat to advance

danger_signals.visual_form: Solid stage obstacles in the constantly scrolling environment
Incoming carriers dropping colored weapon items
danger_signals.meaning: Active enemy presence and combat engagement zones

safety_signals.visual_form: Color-changing items from destroyed enemy carriers (Red, Blue, Green)
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability for weapon upgrade and tactical advantage
Enhanced mobility for evasion and positioning
Life resource replenishment; increased survivability buffer
Tactical safety tool for clearing threats; also triggered passively when taking damage

safety_signals.visual_form: Stage progression markers and respawn locations
safety_signals.meaning: Progression safety net; player can recover from mistakes within reasonable bounds

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: CATS organization; space pirate faction identity
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Various enemy types and boss entities that manifest CATS military forces; cyborg aesthetic implied
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Space colonies under CATS control represent faction-controlled territory; mothership destruction signals CATS presence and military capability

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Federation authority; protagonist Trent represents Federation interests
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player-controlled ZIG spacecraft represents Federation military capability
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Japanese/Federation space colonies serve as contested territory; mothership represents Federation military infrastructure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: CATS breaks covenant and assumes control of Federation space colonies; shift from peace to active conflict
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: CATS military aggression escalates; enemies increase in density and firepower; boss encounters represent major tactical operations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Constantly scrolling environment through eight increasingly difficult levels; visual environment changes per stage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy density and bullet patterns intensify; boss difficulty scales; player must defeat stage boss to liberate that sector

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Weapon color system (Red Cannon, Blue Laser, Green Homing) reflects current loadout; visual weapon upgrade indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Equipped weapon type determines firepower, range, and effectiveness; weapon downgrades after taking damage signal vulnerability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss appearance at stage end; distinct visual entity different from standard enemies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss requires specific tactical engagement; stage scrolling stops; boss-specific attack patterns and movement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Loss of all lives; game loop reset or continue screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must insert credits to continue (arcade context); game returns to stage one on loop continuation

environmental_storytelling.example: Eight levels with increasingly difficult visual scenarios representing deeper penetration into CATS-controlled territory toward final confrontation
Destroyed Federation mothership establishes why Trent is sole operative; loss of Federation military infrastructure justifies desperate solo mission
Japanese/Federation space colonies shown as CATS-controlled territory; visual differentiation between stages implies different colonial regions under occupation
Loop system with increasing difficulty conveys expanding CATS military mobilization and desperation of conflict
Color-coded weapon types (Red, Blue, Green) suggest modular Federation arsenal design; upgradeable system implies Federation maintained advanced military technology

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responsive control through scrolling environment; can grab enemies with Seizer Beam; destroys enemy carriers to collect weapons
Firepower and speed reset to original state; bomb module triggered automatically on hit (secondary defense)
Spawn with pattern-based projectile fire; release weapon carriers when destroyed; increase fire density in later loops
Unique visual appearance; stage-blocking presence; advanced attack patterns; must be defeated to progress
Maintains control of space colonies; breaks peace covenant; coordinates military operations across eight territorial sectors

notes: Zero Wing uses minimal narrative communication through gameplay; world-state is conveyed primarily through enemy density escalation, visual stage progression, and weapon system feedback. The game's political situation (broken peace treaty, territory occupation, liberation mission) is established through synopsis text rather than in-game visuals, typical of early 1990s arcade design. Danger/safety signals rely on arcade conventions: color-coded power-ups, projectile patterns, and boss encounters. Faction identity is implicit in space pirate aesthetic (CATS) versus Federation military context (player) rather than explicit visual markers like heraldry or faction colors.
